name: dep-hash emits GLOBAL for a spec that references no substrate, so the runner falls back to the conservative whole-tree hash
command:
mkdir -p r/rubric/runners r/rubric/detectors r/generated-code-quality-standards/x s && printf 'echo A; bash "$PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/toolB.sh"
' > r/rubric/runners/toolA.sh && printf 'echo B
' > r/rubric/runners/toolB.sh && printf 'echo unrelated
' > r/rubric/detectors/unrelated.sh && printf 'echo enforce; reads generated-code-quality-standards rules
' > r/rubric/runners/enforceX.sh && printf 'id: r1
' > r/generated-code-quality-standards/x/r.yaml && printf '%s' '{"command":"x $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/toolA.sh"}' > s/unit-a.json && printf '%s' '{"command":"x $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/enforceX.sh"}' > s/enforce.json && printf '%s' '{"command":"echo hi with no substrate ref"}' > s/noref.json && node "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/evals/dep-hash.js" r s out.txt 2>/dev/null; g(){ awk -F'	' -v n="$1" '$1==n{print $2}' out.txt; }; [ "$(g noref)" = "GLOBAL" ] && echo global-for-no-ref 1>&2
